reason: All four round-2 gaps substantively resolved. Medium gap #1 (count reconciliation) now has a complete explanatory block in the file header stating 45 unique inventory names vs 24 Code↔Test + 25 Backward Mapping matrix entries, explaining the 21-test FILE-vs-FUNCTION aggregation gap, and directing P3 (build_trace.py) to reconcile by reading actual test files. Low gap #2 (NFR-01 wording) is now in clear English with no Chinese characters. Low gap #3 (NFR-02 section pin) uses fully section-qualified references: cross_cutting.security::test_redteam_shell_true_absent_in_codebase and fr_tests.FR-01.unit::test_fr01_submit_injection_chars_returns_two. Low gap #4 (structural asymmetry) is documented as INTENTIONAL with rationale (NFRs are property/quality gates, not functional scope). Earlier-round fixes preserved: 45 unique names, no NFR-*/cross_cutting.* duplication, format_version bumped to 1.1. YAML structure is valid (proper indentation, comment syntax, no syntax errors).

Previous Sub-Task B-2 review JSON — TEST_INVENTORY.yaml

gaps.message: Structural asymmetry header comment states 'NFR sections are top-level keys NFR-XX.{unit,integration}' but in the actual YAML, NFR-01/NFR-02/NFR-03 are indented at the same level as FR-01/FR-02/FR-03 — i.e., nested under fr_tests, not at the document root. The intent (NFRs are not 'functional scope' and don't need nfr_tests.* nesting) is clearly communicated, but the 'top-level keys' wording is factually inaccurate against the YAML structure. Suggest rewording to 'NFR sections are placed as siblings of FR-XX under fr_tests (not promoted to a nfr_tests.* parent), because NFRs are property/quality gates rather than functional scope'.
